papers.title: Wheat gibberellin oxidase genes and their functions in regulating tillering
papers.authors: Wang T, Li J, Jiang Y, Zhang J, Ni Y, Zhang P, Yao Z, Jiao Z, Li H, Li L, Niu Y, Li Q, Yin G, Niu J

papers.title: Function and transcript analysis of gibberellin-biosynthetic enzymes in wheat
papers.authors: Appleford NEJ, Evans DJ, Lenton JR, Gaskin P, Croker SJ, Devos KM, Phillips AL, Hedden P

papers.title: GIBBERELLIN 3-OXIDASE genes regulate height and grain size in bread wheat
papers.authors: Phillips AL, Huttly AK, Alarcón-Reverte R, et al

edges.mechanism: GA 20-oxidase catalyzes GA12→GA9 and GA53→GA20 conversions; major regulatory site of bioactive GA production; expressed in elongating nodes/ears
edges.evidence_sentence: GA 20-oxidase and 3-oxidase are major sites of regulation in GA biosynthesis; enzymes catalyze multi-step reactions, including conversions of GA12-GA9, and GA53-GA20

edges.mechanism: GA 3-oxidase catalyses 3β-hydroxylation of GA20 to GA1 (bioactive); TaGA3ox2 essential for normal development
edges.evidence_sentence: GA3OX3 catalyses the 3β-hydroxylation of GA20 to GA1

edges.mechanism: Negative GA feedback: GA3 treatment reduces TaGA20ox-A2/B2 expression
edges.evidence_sentence: exogenous GA3 reduces the expressions of GA20oxs and GA3oxs, and increases the expressions of GA2oxs

edges.mechanism: Negative feedback: GA3 decreases TaGA3ox-B2/D2 expression
Positive feedforward: GA3 increases TaGA2ox expression (GA catabolism homeostatic loop)
edges.evidence_sentence: exogenous GA3 increases the expressions of GA2oxs

edges.mechanism: DELLA triggers GA biosynthesis upregulation (negative feedback when DELLA accumulates); Rht-B1 stabilized in dwarf → elevated GA biosynthesis genes
edges.evidence_sentence: DELLA protein signaling alters GA oxidase gene transcription through GA-GID1-DELLA tricomplex

edges.mechanism: ga3ox2 triple mutant severely dwarfed; bioactive GA1 deficient
edges.evidence_sentence: ga3ox2 triple mutant: severely dwarfed, infertile, compact spikes with fewer spikelets

edges.mechanism: GA1ox1-B1 catalyses 1β-hydroxylation GA9→GA61 and GA20→GA60, removing precursors from bioactive pool; ga1ox1 accumulates bioactive GA
edges.evidence_sentence: GA1OX1-B1: Catalyzes GA9→GA61 and GA20→GA60 (1-hydroxylation preceding 3-hydroxylation); ga1ox1 Mutant: Bioactive GA1/GA4 extremely high; 10.7 cm taller in 2021 field trial

perturbations.evidence_sentence: ga3ox2 triple mutant: GA1 levels ~3-fold lower than wild-type in seedlings; severely dwarfed, infertile
perturbations.notes: Triple homeolog mutant; GA3 rescue restores height and fertility

perturbations.evidence_sentence: ga3ox3 Mutants: 3.0-4.5 cm shorter than wild-type; grain width/TGW reduced
perturbations.notes: Field phenotype: all 4 lowest internodes shorter

perturbations.evidence_sentence: ga1ox1 Mutant: Bioactive GA1/GA4 extremely high; 10.7 cm taller in 2021 field trial; 1.2 cm taller in 2023
perturbations.notes: Loss blocks GA catabolism by-path; increases bioactive GA pool

perturbations.evidence_sentence: twice-weekly sprays of 10 μM GA3 almost completely restored the height and fertility
continuous GA3 treatment significantly increased the tiller number
perturbations.notes: Exogenous GA3 spray on wild-type wheat
